content:7d6c8d4a1cb51faf008f5bf1ffb24838.title: He helped others again and again, then he was left paralysed
content:7d6c8d4a1cb51faf008f5bf1ffb24838.english_reader_card: A homeless man in Bolton who returned a lost handbag and gave fundraiser money to others was later attacked and left paralysed.

Months later, he was attacked, suffered a broken spine, developed sepsis, and was left paralysed from the waist down after surgery at Salford Royal Hospital.

english_fact_card.who_affected: Paul Calderbank, 54, and his family.

english_fact_card.what_happened: A female swan was killed by a car near Queensmere Dam in Swinton, Salford, in front of her mate and six cygnets. The car, possibly a Ford, did not stop. Police are investigating and have appealed for public help.

english_fact_card.who_affected: The swan family and local community; Salford Police investigating
english_fact_card.why_now: Police issued a statement and public appeal on 26 June 2026, and the story is spreading on social media.

english_fact_card.reader_action: Contact Salford Police if you have information about the incident.

content:e3d91c42f326773867f3b065f0074e90.english_reader_card: Two Bolton men have been jailed and face deportation after police found a large cannabis farm on Newport Street. The raid was in December 2025, and sentencing was reported on 26 June.

english_fact_card.what_happened: Two men, Ridgan Taga and Leonard Tota, were jailed and face deportation after a large-scale cannabis grow was found in Bolton. Police raided a property on Newport Street on 23 December 2025, uncovering a professionally adapted cannabis farm with bypassed electricity.
english_fact_card.when: Raid on 23 December 2025; sentencing reported 26 June 2026
english_fact_card.who_affected: Ridgan Taga and Leonard Tota, both 26, of St George's Road, Bolton; Tota sentenced to 14 months for cannabis production; Taga sentenced for production, supply, and possession of criminal property.
english_fact_card.why_now: Sentencing and deportation announcement reported on 26 June 2026.
english_fact_card.reader_value: Update on a local drug operation with sentencing and deportation outcome.

Walkers are objecting, saying the bridge should be repaired or replaced instead of closed.
english_fact_card.where: Quarry Bank, Styal, Cheshire (near Greater Manchester border)

english_fact_card.who_affected: Walkers, local residents, National Trust members, and visitors to the Quarry Bank estate.
english_fact_card.why_now: The planning application has been made public, and a campaign to save the bridge is gaining momentum.
english_fact_card.reader_value: Local planning dispute affecting public access to a popular walking route.
english_fact_card.reader_action: Check the planning application on Cheshire East Council's portal if you wish to comment.

content:d56d641846e193d02a9003098a9c528c.english_reader_card: Paul Sharpe, 56, from Stockport, has admitted kidnapping a 15-year-old girl at knifepoint in Altrincham in May and sexually assaulting her during an eight-hour ordeal; he will be sentenced on 4 September.

english_fact_card.what_happened: Convicted murderer Paul Sharpe, 56, from Stockport, pleaded guilty to kidnapping a 15-year-old girl at knifepoint in Altrincham on 18 May, tying her up, forcing her to drink alcohol, and sexually assaulting her over eight hours. He was arrested in Knutsford, Cheshire, after police CCTV inquiries. He admitted six offences including kidnapping, sexual assault, and threats with a blade.

english_fact_card.when: Incident on 18 May 2026; guilty plea on 3 July 2026; sentencing set for 4 September 2026

english_fact_card.reader_value: Informs the public about a serious crime and its legal progression, relevant for community awareness.
